**UTC+03:00** is an identifier for a [time offset from UTC](/wiki/UTC_offset "UTC offset") of +03:00. In areas using this time offset, the time is three hours ahead of the [Coordinated Universal Time (UTC)](/wiki/Coordinated_Universal_Time "Coordinated Universal Time").
Following the [ISO 8601](/wiki/ISO_8601 "ISO 8601") standard, a time with this offset would be written as, for example, 2019-02-08T23:36:06+03:00.

#### Arabia Standard Time

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=UTC%2B03:00&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Arabia Standard Time")]

For other time zones with the abbreviation AST, see [AST (time)](/wiki/AST_(disambiguation)#Time_zones "AST (disambiguation)").

Arabia Standard Time, or AST (indicated by *KSA* on some Arabic TV stations), is used by some of the countries in the [Middle East](/wiki/Middle_East "Middle East"). As this time zone is predominantly in the equatorial region, there is no significant change in day length throughout the year, so daylight saving time is not observed. Between 1982 and 2007, Iraq observed *Arabia Daylight Time* (UTC+04:00) but the government abolished DST in March 2008.[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6)

Arabia Standard Time is used by the following countries:

* Bahrain
* Iraq
* Jordan
* Kuwait
* Qatar
* Saudi Arabia[[a]](#cite_note-7)
* Syria
* Yemen

## Notes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=UTC%2B03:00&action=edit&section=19 "Edit section: Notes")]

1. The westernmost point at UTC+03:00 is the westernmost point of contiguous Russia, near [Lavry](/wiki/Lavry "Lavry"), [Pskov Oblast](/wiki/Pskov_Oblast "Pskov Oblast") (27°19' E). The [time zone](/wiki/Time_zone "Time zone") employed there (corresponding to [45°E](/wiki/60th_meridian_east "60th meridian east")) is 17°41' E of physical time, i.e. roughly 1 hour and 11 minutes ahead of [physical time](/wiki/Physical_time "Physical time"), making for the largest overall discrepancy between time used and physical time for UTC+03:00.
2. The easternmost point at UTC+03:00 is [Cape Zhelaniya](/wiki/Cape_Zhelaniya "Cape Zhelaniya"), [Severny Island](/wiki/Severny_Island "Severny Island"), [Novaya Zemlya](/wiki/Novaya_Zemlya "Novaya Zemlya"), Russia (69°06' E). The time zone employed there (corresponding to [45°E](/wiki/45th_meridian_east "45th meridian east")) is 24°06' W of physical time, i.e. roughly 1 hour and 36 minutes behind physical time, making for the largest discrepancy between time used and physical time for UTC+03:00.
3. On February 8, 2011, [Russian president](/wiki/President_of_Russia "President of Russia") [Dmitry Medvedev](/wiki/Dmitry_Medvedev "Dmitry Medvedev") issued a decree cancelling [daylight saving time](/wiki/Daylight_saving_time "Daylight saving time") in Russia. Under the decree, all clocks in Russia advanced by 1 hour on March 27, 2011, but did not change back the following October, effectively making Kaliningrad Time UTC+03:00 permanently, and Moscow Time [UTC+04:00](/wiki/UTC%2B04:00 "UTC+04:00") permanently. This proved unpopular because of the dark mornings, children walking to school and people going to work in complete darkness. On October 26, 2014, Russia permanently returned to [standard time](/wiki/Standard_time "Standard time") by setting the clocks back by 1 hour effectively making Kaliningrad Time [UTC+02:00](/wiki/UTC%2B02:00 "UTC+02:00") permanently and Moscow Time UTC+03:00 permanently.
4. Ukraine had UTC+02:00 plus regularly [EEST](/wiki/EEST "EEST") from 1990 till 2011 (in years 1981–1990 Moscow Summer Time) until the [Ukrainian parliament](/wiki/Ukrainian_parliament "Ukrainian parliament") added 1 hour "on the territory of Ukraine from March 27, 2011" and canceled daylight saving time on September 20, 2011, *de facto* making EEST (UTC+03:00) the new standard time.[[16]](#cite_note-Kyivwintertimeisgone-17) After strong criticism from the mass media, on 18 October 2011 the Ukrainian parliament cancelled its previous decision.[[17]](#cite_note-Ukraine-back-to-winter-time-18)
